narrative:
On June 10, 2026, the Karnataka High Court listed 2,932 cases across 47 judges, hearing 1,255 to achieve an overall hearing rate of 42.8%. This performance represents a slight decline in efficiency compared to the previous day (June 9), which recorded a 47.5% hearing rate for 2,687 scheduled cases, but remains marginally higher than the 41.0% rate observed on June 8.

Judicial performance varied widely under heavy docket pressure. Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19 managed the largest single docket of 445 cases, hearing 78 of them (17.5%). In contrast, Justice V Srishananda demonstrated strong throughput, hearing 71 of 96 scheduled matters (74.0%). Justice H.P. Sandesh also converted efficiently, hearing 53 of 69 cases (76.8%) in his main block. Conversely, Justice B M Shyam Prasad resolved up to 81.2% in his smaller sessions but left a large block of 128 cases entirely untouched.

In terms of hearing stages, Orders dominated court schedules with 1,067 listed cases, converting at 46.5%. Admission and Preliminary Hearings converted at 44.9% and 42.2% respectively, while complex Final Hearings registered a lower conversion rate of 21.5% (117 of 543 cases). Case-type distribution was heavily weighted toward Writ Petitions, which represented 1,470 listed cases but saw a conversion rate of only 37.3%. More favorable clearance rates were observed in Criminal Revision Petitions (CRL.RP), which cleared 85.5% of 55 scheduled matters, and Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA), converting at 54.5% of 290 cases.
